# CONVENTIONS.md

This file is the authoritative source of structured and mechanical conventions this
project follows. Each block states a rule with a stable citable ID that reviewers and
the AI agent reference in commits, PR descriptions, and code reviews.

These conventions are the scaffolder's baseline rule selection for the vetted
Dioxus-fullstack PWA skeleton (grounded in the Camerata principle library's
`rust:dioxus` domain). This is a **starting baseline, not a final review** — once the
Architect orchestrator's autonomous rule-review step exists (see
`docs/plans/2026-07-09_product-owner-head-vibe-mode.md`), it re-reviews the full
corpus against this app's actual requirements and refines this file. Cite a rule's ID
when applying it (for example, per RUST-DIOXUS-9).

The companion file AGENTS.md holds this project's prose-enforcement directives.

The `RUST-DIOXUS-*` and `ARCH-STRUCTURED-ERRORS-1` IDs below are pulled verbatim from
the shared Camerata principle library. The two `### CUSTOM-*` blocks below
(`CUSTOM-db-on-demand`, `CUSTOM-pwa-auto-capture`) are NOT corpus rules — they are
architect-authored CUSTOM rules (Camerata's first-class `CustomRule` feature),
specific to decisions this scaffolder template makes rather than a general Rust or
Dioxus stance. When a Camerata project is registered for this scaffolded app (the
`POST /api/apps` flow), these two are seeded into the project's ruleset verbatim, so
this file and the project's ruleset stay in sync from birth.

---

### RUST-DIOXUS-9 — Server-to-client calls: framework server functions
The UI reaches the server exclusively through the `#[server]` macro (`src/server_fns.rs`). A server function's client stub and server handler are generated from one definition, so there is one source of truth per operation. Any call to a third party (payment provider, email gateway, external API) lives in a server function's server-side body, never in client-side (`components`/`pages`) code. Direct use of `reqwest`, `fetch`, or any other HTTP client in wasm-frontend code is forbidden — this is the rule that keeps the wasm build clean (see `Cargo.toml`'s comment on the `wasm32` target block). Note the companion mechanism this depends on: `#[server]` decides which half (real body vs. network stub) to compile via the `server` / `web` Cargo features (`Cargo.toml`), not target-arch alone — building one platform without disabling the other's feature (`--no-default-features --features <platform>`, as `.github/workflows/ci.yml` does) risks the real server-side body ending up in the wasm bundle.

### RUST-DIOXUS-11 — Single binary serves SSR, the client bundle, and server functions
One native binary (`src/main.rs`'s `#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]` branch, wired in `src/server.rs`) serves the server-rendered shell, the wasm client bundle, and every server-function endpoint. No separate frontend process, no separate API binary. There is no database in this process (DB-on-demand — see below); adding one later is a separate service/module, not a rewrite of this rule.

### RUST-DIOXUS-14 — Common UI elements have one canonical implementation
`src/components/` is the design system's primitives layer (`AppShell`, `Card`, `Button`, `Field`). Pages (`src/pages/`) compose these; a page never reimplements a primitive inline. Palette, spacing, radii, and focus rings live in `assets/design/tokens.css` + `assets/design/components.css` — a design change is an edit to those two files, not a codebase-wide search-and-replace.

### RUST-DIOXUS-1 — File-role layout
The crate is organized by what a file does: page-level components under `pages`, reusable primitives under `components`, server-reachable operations under `server_fns.rs`, native-only server wiring under `server.rs` (cfg-gated out of the wasm build), the wasm-only auto-capture bridge under `wasm_bridge.rs`. The route table (`Route`) lives in `routes.rs` at the crate's top.

### ARCH-STRUCTURED-ERRORS-1 — Server functions return typed errors, not stringly-typed ones
Server functions return `Result<T, ServerFnError>`; a server function that wraps a real failure mode (validation, a downstream call) constructs a specific, named error rather than a bare string, so a caller can pattern-match rather than substring-match a message.

### CUSTOM-db-on-demand _(custom · domain: *)_
No database until a requirement needs one. This skeleton ships with no Postgres, no `sqlx`, no migrations, and no ORM. Persistence is added by a later phase only when the app's actual requirements need it (see `AppRequirements::needs_persistence` in the scaffolder) — never speculatively.

### CUSTOM-pwa-auto-capture _(custom · domain: *)_
The auto-capture reporter is never removed or bypassed. `assets/error-reporter.js` (window.onerror / unhandledrejection / failed-fetch) and `src/wasm_bridge.rs` (the Rust panic hook) together catch runtime defects before a user has to report them, and POST a `DefectReport`-shaped payload to the capture endpoint. New code must not swallow errors in a way that keeps them from reaching these listeners (e.g. a blanket `try/catch` around the whole render tree with no re-throw), and must not remove the reporter to "clean up" the entrypoint.

### CI hygiene
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `cargo check` / `cargo test` on every push and PR, plus the governance check declared there. A change that breaks either is not mergeable.
